From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Dedicated event to screen films

"Grand Jury Prize" redirects here. For the specific instance, see [Grand Jury Prize (Venice Film Festival)](/wiki/Grand_Jury_Prize_(Venice_Film_Festival) "Grand Jury Prize (Venice Film Festival)").

|  |
| --- |
| Film festivals |
| * [List of film festivals](/wiki/List_of_film_festivals "List of film festivals")   + [Europe](/wiki/List_of_film_festivals_in_Europe "List of film festivals in Europe")   + [North/Central America](/wiki/List_of_film_festivals_in_North_and_Central_America "List of film festivals in North and Central America")   + [Oceania](/wiki/List_of_film_festivals_in_Oceania "List of film festivals in Oceania") * [Fantastic and horror film festivals](/wiki/List_of_fantastic_and_horror_film_festivals "List of fantastic and horror film festivals") * [Documentary film festivals](/wiki/Documentary_film_festivals "Documentary film festivals") * [Human rights film festivals](/wiki/List_of_human_rights_film_festivals "List of human rights film festivals") * [LGBT film festivals](/wiki/List_of_LGBT_film_festivals "List of LGBT film festivals") * [New Media art festivals](/wiki/New_Media_art_festivals "New Media art festivals") |

A **film festival** is an organized, extended presentation of [films](/wiki/Film "Film") in one or more [cinemas](/wiki/Movie_theater "Movie theater") or screening venues, usually annually and in a single city or region. Some film festivals show films outdoors or online.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

Films may be of recent date and, depending on the festival's focus, can include both international and domestic releases. Some film festivals focus on a specific format of film, such as [documentary](/wiki/Documentary_film "Documentary film"), or runtime, such as [short film](/wiki/Short_film "Short film") festivals, or genre, such as [horror films](/wiki/Horror_film "Horror film"), category of filmmakers, such as [women](/wiki/Woman "Woman"), production country/region or subject matter.

Film festivals can be competitive or non-competitive, and are often regarded within the film industry as launchpads for new filmmakers and indie films, as well as boosters for established filmmakers and studio productions. The films are either invited by festival curators or selected by festival programmers from submissions made by the filmmakers, film producers, production companies, sales agents, or distributors. Often, audiences have the opportunity to watch films premiering months before their commercial release, or films that may not benefit from a wide release and would otherwise be hard to find.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

The oldest film festival in the world is the [Venice Film Festival](/wiki/Venice_Film_Festival "Venice Film Festival").[[3]](#cite_note-:0-3) The most prestigious film festivals in the world, known as the "Big Five", are (listed chronologically according to the date of foundation): [Venice](/wiki/Venice_Film_Festival "Venice Film Festival"), [Cannes](/wiki/Cannes_Film_Festival "Cannes Film Festival"), [Berlin](/wiki/Berlin_International_Film_Festival "Berlin International Film Festival") (the original *Big Three*), [Toronto](/wiki/Toronto_International_Film_Festival "Toronto International Film Festival"), and [Sundance](/wiki/Sundance_Film_Festival "Sundance Film Festival"). Other major festivals include [Karlovy Vary](/wiki/Karlovy_Vary_International_Film_Festival "Karlovy Vary International Film Festival"), [Locarno](/wiki/Locarno_Film_Festival "Locarno Film Festival"), [San Sebastián](/wiki/San_Sebasti%C3%A1n_International_Film_Festival "San Sebastián International Film Festival"), [SXSW](/wiki/SXSW "SXSW"), [Telluride](/wiki/Telluride_Film_Festival "Telluride Film Festival"), [Tribeca](/wiki/Tribeca_Festival "Tribeca Festival"), [Raindance Film Festival](/wiki/Raindance_Film_Festival "Raindance Film Festival"), [Edinburgh Film Festival](/wiki/Edinburgh_Film_Festival "Edinburgh Film Festival"), [Glasgow Film Festival](/wiki/Glasgow_Film_Festival "Glasgow Film Festival"), [Slamdance Film Festival](/wiki/Slamdance_Film_Festival "Slamdance Film Festival"), [Cinequest Film & Creativity Festival](/wiki/Cinequest_Film_%26_Creativity_Festival "Cinequest Film & Creativity Festival"), and the three largest and most prestigious genre festivals, [Sitges](/wiki/Sitges_Film_Festival "Sitges Film Festival"), [Fantasia](/wiki/Fantasia_International_Film_Festival "Fantasia International Film Festival") and [Fantastic Fest](/wiki/Fantastic_Fest "Fantastic Fest").[[4]](#cite_note-:1-4)[[5]](#cite_note-dearcinema-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6)[[7]](#cite_note-7)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Film_festival&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/94/66%C3%A8me_Festival_de_Venise_%28Mostra%29_Palais_du_Cinema.jpg/250px-66%C3%A8me_Festival_de_Venise_%28Mostra%29_Palais_du_Cinema.jpg)](/wiki/File:66%C3%A8me_Festival_de_Venise_(Mostra)_Palais_du_Cinema.jpg)

The [Venice Film Festival](/wiki/Venice_Film_Festival "Venice Film Festival"), the oldest film festival in the world and one of the most prestigious and publicized

The [Venice Film Festival](/wiki/Venice_Film_Festival "Venice Film Festival") in [Italy](/wiki/Italy "Italy") began in 1932 and is the oldest film festival still running.[[3]](#cite_note-:0-3)

[Mainland Europe](/wiki/Mainland_Europe "Mainland Europe")'s biggest independent film festival is [ÉCU The European Independent Film Festival](/wiki/%C3%89CU_The_European_Independent_Film_Festival "ÉCU The European Independent Film Festival"), which started in 2006 and takes place every spring in Paris, France.[[8]](#cite_note-faceoffilm.com-8) [Edinburgh International Film Festival](/wiki/Edinburgh_International_Film_Festival "Edinburgh International Film Festival") is the longest-running festival in [Great Britain](/wiki/Great_Britain "Great Britain") as well as the longest continually running film festival in the world.

[Australia](/wiki/Australia "Australia")'s first and longest-running film festival is the [Melbourne International Film Festival](/wiki/Melbourne_International_Film_Festival "Melbourne International Film Festival") (1952), followed by the [Sydney Film Festival](/wiki/Sydney_Film_Festival "Sydney Film Festival") (1954).

[North America](/wiki/North_America "North America")'s first and longest-running short film festival is the [Yorkton Film Festival](/wiki/Yorkton_Film_Festival "Yorkton Film Festival"), established in 1947.[[9]](#cite_note-Yorkton1-9)[[10]](#cite_note-Yorkton2-10)

The 1948 Cleveland, Ohio Film Festival was the first film festival in the United States to recognize educational, industrial, training, travel, and other types of sponsored films.[[11]](#cite_note-11)

The longest continuously running film festival in the United States is the [Columbus International Film & Animation Festival](/wiki/Columbus_International_Film_%26_Animation_Festival "Columbus International Film & Animation Festival"), also known as [The Chris Awards](/wiki/The_Chris_Awards "The Chris Awards"), which began in 1953. According to the Film Arts Foundation in [San Francisco](/wiki/San_Francisco "San Francisco"), " 'The Chris Awards' is one of the most prestigious documentaries, educational, business, and informational competitions in the U.S.; it is the oldest of its kind in North America and celebrating its 54th year". It was followed four years later by the [San Francisco International Film Festival](/wiki/San_Francisco_International_Film_Festival "San Francisco International Film Festival"), held in March 1957, which emphasized feature-length dramatic films. The festival played a major role in introducing foreign films to American audiences. Films in the first year included [Akira Kurosawa](/wiki/Akira_Kurosawa "Akira Kurosawa")'s *[Throne of Blood](/wiki/Throne_of_Blood "Throne of Blood")* and [Satyajit Ray](/wiki/Satyajit_Ray "Satyajit Ray")'s *[Pather Panchali](/wiki/Pather_Panchali_(film) "Pather Panchali (film)")*.

Today, thousands of film festivals take place around the world—from high-profile festivals such as [Sundance Film Festival](/wiki/Sundance_Film_Festival "Sundance Film Festival") ([Park City, Utah](/wiki/Park_City,_Utah "Park City, Utah")), [Newport Beach Film Festival](/wiki/Newport_Beach_Film_Festival "Newport Beach Film Festival") and [Slamdance Film Festival](/wiki/Slamdance_Film_Festival "Slamdance Film Festival"), to horror festivals such as [FrightFest](/wiki/FrightFest_(film_festival) "FrightFest (film festival)"), [Screamfest](/wiki/Screamfest_Horror_Film_Festival "Screamfest Horror Film Festival"), [Telluride Horror Show](/wiki/Telluride_Horror_Show "Telluride Horror Show"), and the Park City Film Music Festival, the first American film festival dedicated to honoring music in film.

Film Funding competitions such as Writers and Filmmakers were introduced when the cost of production could be lowered significantly, and internet technology allowed for the collaboration of film production.

Film festivals have evolved significantly since the [COVID-19 pandemic](/wiki/COVID-19_pandemic "COVID-19 pandemic"). Many festivals opted for virtual or hybrid festivals. The film industry, which was already in upheaval due to streaming options, has faced another major shift, and movies showcased at festivals have an even shorter runway to online launches.

### Sponsored films

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Film_festival&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Sponsored films")]

The 1948 Cleveland Film Festival was the first film festival in the United States to honor educational, industrial, training, travel, and other types of [sponsored films](/wiki/Sponsored_films "Sponsored films").[[27]](#cite_note-27) In June 1948, six hundred people watched one or more of the 65 films screened at the one-day Cleveland Film Festival held in General Electric’s Lighting Institute at [Nela Park](/wiki/Nela_Park "Nela Park").[[28]](#cite_note-28)[[29]](#cite_note-29)

In the second year of the Cleveland Film Festival, 99 [16 mm films](/wiki/16_mm_film "16 mm film") were shown and “Oscars” were awarded in eleven classifications.[[30]](#cite_note-30)

Having an entry, or better yet, winning an award at the Cleveland Film Festival, was a significant event. Ads for studios and dozens of movies appeared in "Business Screen Magazine" in the late 1940s and early 1950s, promoting the film or studio as a winner at the Cleveland Film Festival.

By 1956, sponsored film festivals had become ubiquitous. In addition to dozens of city-sponsored film festivals, many organizations sponsored film festivals.[[31]](#cite_note-31)

In 1957, the Cleveland Film Council, the organizers of the Cleveland Film Festival, ceased operation of the festival. Sponsored film festivals continued on, but never meant as much after that.[[32]](#cite_note-32)

Further information: [Aswan International Women's Film Festival](/wiki/Aswan_International_Women%27s_Film_Festival "Aswan International Women's Film Festival")

A few film festivals have focused on highlighting specific issues, topics, or subjects. These festivals have included mainstream and [independent films](/wiki/Independent_film "Independent film"). Some examples include [military films](/wiki/GI_Film_Festival "GI Film Festival"), [health-related film festivals](/wiki/Public_Health_Film_Festival "Public Health Film Festival"), and [human rights film festivals](/wiki/Human_rights_film_festival "Human rights film festival").

There are festivals, especially in the US, that highlight and promote films made by or about various ethnic groups and nationalities or feature the cinema from a specific foreign country. These include [African-Americans](/wiki/Category:African-American_film_festivals "Category:African-American film festivals"), [Asian-Americans](/wiki/Category:Asian-American_film_festivals "Category:Asian-American film festivals"), [Mexican-Americans](/wiki/Mexican-Americans "Mexican-Americans"), [Arabs](/wiki/Arab_Film_Festival "Arab Film Festival"), [Jews](/wiki/Category:Jewish_film_festivals "Category:Jewish film festivals"), Italian, [German](/wiki/Berlin_%26_Beyond_Film_Festival "Berlin & Beyond Film Festival"), [French](/wiki/VCU_French_Film_Festival "VCU French Film Festival"), [Palestinian](/wiki/DC_Palestinian_Film_and_Arts_Festival "DC Palestinian Film and Arts Festival"), and [Native American](/wiki/Category:Native_American_film_festivals "Category:Native American film festivals"). The [Deauville American Film Festival](/wiki/Deauville_American_Film_Festival "Deauville American Film Festival") in France is devoted to the [cinema of the United States](/wiki/Cinema_of_the_United_States "Cinema of the United States").
[LGBTQ+](/wiki/List_of_LGBT_film_festivals "List of LGBT film festivals") and [Women's film festivals](/wiki/Women%27s_film_festival "Women's film festival") are also popular.

### North American film festivals

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Film_festival&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: North American film festivals")]

[Tribeca Festival](/wiki/Tribeca_Festival "Tribeca Festival"), one of the most prestigious in North America, ranks first worldwide in terms of audience attendance and 11th in terms of media attendance.[[33]](#cite_note-33)

The [San Francisco International Film Festival](/wiki/San_Francisco_International_Film_Festival "San Francisco International Film Festival"), founded by Irving "Bud" Levin in 1957, is the oldest continuous annual film festival in the United States. It highlights current trends in international [filmmaking](/wiki/Filmmaking "Filmmaking") and [video production](/wiki/Video_production "Video production") with an emphasis on work that has not yet secured American distribution.[[34]](#cite_note-34)

The [Newport Beach Film Festival](/wiki/Newport_Beach_Film_Festival "Newport Beach Film Festival"), founded by Gregg Schwenk in 1999, has emerged as the largest international cinema event in coastal [Southern California](/wiki/Southern_California "Southern California"), attracting over 56,000 attendees to [Orange County, California](/wiki/Orange_County,_California "Orange County, California"). The Festival partners with over 40 [non-profit organizations](/wiki/Nonprofit_organization "Nonprofit organization") and pairs each with a film that aligns with their mission. The films featured include World, North America, U.S., and West Coast premieres, as well as the International Spotlight Series, which celebrates foreign language films.

The [Vancouver International Film Festival](/wiki/Vancouver_International_Film_Festival "Vancouver International Film Festival"), founded in 1958, is one of the largest film festivals in North America. It focuses on East Asian films, Canadian films, and nonfiction films. In 2016, the audience reached 133,000 and the festival featured 324 films.

The [Toronto International Film Festival](/wiki/Toronto_International_Film_Festival "Toronto International Film Festival"), founded by Bill Marshall, Henk Van der Kolk, and Dusty Cohl,[[35]](#cite_note-35) is one of North America's most important film festivals, and is the most widely attended.[[36]](#cite_note-36)

The [Chicago International Film Festival](/wiki/Chicago_International_Film_Festival "Chicago International Film Festival"), founded in 1964, is North America's longest-running competitive film festival. The 60th Chicago International Film Festival, scheduled for October, will host over 40,000 attendees from around the world. The Festival's program, screening 175+ films from more than 50 countries, is presented in sections including the International Competition, New Directors Competition, Documentary, Black Perspectives, City & State, and Special Presentations.

The [Cleveland International Film Festival](/wiki/Cleveland_International_Film_Festival "Cleveland International Film Festival") (CIFF), founded in 1977, is the largest film festival in [Ohio](/wiki/Ohio "Ohio") and among the longest-running film festivals in the United States.[[37]](#cite_note-37) The film festival is held at the [Playhouse Square](/wiki/Playhouse_Square "Playhouse Square"), which are a series of elegant theaters built in the early 1920s, and the largest performing arts center in the United States outside of [New York City](/wiki/New_York_City "New York City") (only [Lincoln Center](/wiki/Lincoln_Center "Lincoln Center") is larger).[[38]](#cite_note-38)

The [Ottawa Canadian Film Festival](/wiki/Ottawa_Canadian_Film_Festival "Ottawa Canadian Film Festival"), abbreviated OCanFilmFest, was co-founded by Ottawa-based filmmakers Jith Paul, Ed Kucerak, and Blair Campbell in 2015. It features films of various durations and genres from filmmakers across Canada.

The [Sundance Film Festival](/wiki/Sundance_film_festival "Sundance film festival") founded by [Sterling Van Wagenen](/wiki/Sterling_Van_Wagenen "Sterling Van Wagenen") (then head of Wildwood, Robert Redford's company), John Earle, and Cirina Hampton Catania (both serving on the Utah Film Commission at the time) is a significant festival for independent film.

The [Woodstock Film Festival](/wiki/Woodstock_Film_Festival "Woodstock Film Festival") was launched in 2000 by filmmakers Meira Blaustein and Laurent Rejto to bring high-quality, independent films to the [Hudson Valley](/wiki/Hudson_Valley "Hudson Valley") region of [New York](/wiki/New_York_(state) "New York (state)"). In 2010, [Indiewire](/wiki/Indiewire "Indiewire") named the Woodstock Film Festival among the top 50 independent film festivals worldwide.[[39]](#cite_note-39)[[40]](#cite_note-40)

The [Regina International Film Festival and Awards (RIFFA)](/wiki/Regina_International_Film_Festival "Regina International Film Festival")[[41]](#cite_note-riffa.ca-41)[[42]](#cite_note-42)[[43]](#cite_note-43)[[44]](#cite_note-leaderpost.com-44) founded by John Thimothy,[[44]](#cite_note-leaderpost.com-44) one of the top 5 leading international film festivals in western Canada ([Regina, Saskatchewan](/wiki/Regina,_Saskatchewan "Regina, Saskatchewan")) also been referred to as *“The People's Festival of Canada”*, a phrase highlighting its identity rooted in inclusivity, accessibility, and community wellbeing [[41]](#cite_note-riffa.ca-41).

Toronto's [Hot Docs](/wiki/Hot_Docs_Canadian_International_Documentary_Festival "Hot Docs Canadian International Documentary Festival"), founded by filmmaker Paul Jay, is a North American documentary film festival. Toronto has the largest number of film festivals in the world,[[45]](#cite_note-45) ranging from cultural, independent, and historic films.

The [Seattle International Film Festival](/wiki/Seattle_International_Film_Festival "Seattle International Film Festival"), which screens 270 features and approximately 150 short films, is the largest American film festival in terms of the number of feature productions.[[14]](#cite_note-:5-14)

The [Expresión en Corto International Film Festival](/wiki/Expresi%C3%B3n_en_Corto_International_Film_Festival "Expresión en Corto International Film Festival") is the largest competitive film festival in [Mexico](/wiki/Mexico "Mexico"). It specializes in emerging talent and is held in the last week of each July in the two colonial cities of [San Miguel de Allende](/wiki/San_Miguel_de_Allende "San Miguel de Allende") and [Guanajuato](/wiki/Guanajuato "Guanajuato").

Other Mexican festivals include the [Guadalajara International Film Festival](/wiki/Guadalajara_International_Film_Festival "Guadalajara International Film Festival") in [Guadalajara](/wiki/Guadalajara "Guadalajara"), [Oaxaca Film Fest](/wiki/Oaxaca_Film_Fest "Oaxaca Film Fest"), the [Morelia International Film Festival](/wiki/Morelia_International_Film_Festival "Morelia International Film Festival") in Morelia, Michoacan [Mexico](/wiki/Mexico "Mexico"), and the [Los Cabos International Film Festival](/wiki/Los_Cabos_International_Film_Festival "Los Cabos International Film Festival") founded by [Scott Cross](/wiki/Scott_Cross_(film_director) "Scott Cross (film director)"), [Sean Cross](/wiki/Sean_Cross "Sean Cross"), and Eduardo Sanchez Navarro, in [Los Cabos](/wiki/Los_Cabos "Los Cabos"), [Baja Sur](/wiki/Baja_Sur "Baja Sur"), Mexico are considered[*[by whom?](/wiki/Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Words_to_watch#Unsupported_attributions "Wikipedia:Manual of Style/Words to watch")*] the most important film festivals in Latin America. In 2015, [*Variety*](/wiki/Variety_(magazine) "Variety (magazine)") called the [Los Cabos International Film Festival](/wiki/Los_Cabos_International_Film_Festival "Los Cabos International Film Festival") the "Cannes of Latin America".[[46]](#cite_note-46)

### The Caribbean

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Film_festival&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: The Caribbean")]

For Spanish-speaking countries, the Dominican International Film Festival occurs annually in Puerto Plata, [Dominican Republic](/wiki/Dominican_Republic "Dominican Republic"). [Tampere Film Festival](/wiki/Tampere_Film_Festival "Tampere Film Festival"), an international festival for [short films](/wiki/Short_film "Short film"), in 2011

Further information: [List of film festivals in Europe](/wiki/List_of_film_festivals_in_Europe "List of film festivals in Europe")

The most important European film festivals are the [Venice Film Festival](/wiki/Venice_Film_Festival "Venice Film Festival") (late summer to early autumn), the [Cannes Film Festival](/wiki/Cannes_Film_Festival "Cannes Film Festival") (late spring to early summer), and the [Berlin International Film Festival](/wiki/Berlin_International_Film_Festival "Berlin International Film Festival") (late winter to early spring), founded in 1932, 1946, and 1951, respectively. The [Edinburgh International Film Festival](/wiki/Edinburgh_International_Film_Festival "Edinburgh International Film Festival"), founded in 1946, is the world's oldest continually running film festival.

### Animation

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Film_festival&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Animation")]

Many film festivals are dedicated exclusively to animation.

* [Annecy International Animated Film Festival](/wiki/Annecy_International_Animated_Film_Festival "Annecy International Animated Film Festival") (f. 1960—the oldest)
* [Zagreb](/wiki/Animafest_Zagreb "Animafest Zagreb") (f. 1972)
* [Ottawa](/wiki/Ottawa_International_Animation_Festival "Ottawa International Animation Festival") (f. 1976)
* [Hiroshima](/wiki/Hiroshima_International_Animation_Festival "Hiroshima International Animation Festival") (f. 1985)
* [KROK](/wiki/KROK_International_Animated_Films_Festival "KROK International Animated Films Festival") (f. 1989)
* [Anima Mundi](/wiki/Anima_Mundi_(event) "Anima Mundi (event)") (f. 1992)
* Fredrikstad Animation Festival (f. 1994)
* [Animac](/wiki/Animac "Animac") (f. 1996)
* [ASIFAC Animation Festival and Conference](https://www.asifa-south.com/asifac-festival) (f. 2017)

Various regional festivals occur in various countries. The [Austin Film Festival](/wiki/Austin_Film_Festival "Austin Film Festival") is accredited by the [Academy of Motion Picture Arts & Sciences](/wiki/Academy_of_Motion_Picture_Arts_%26_Sciences "Academy of Motion Picture Arts & Sciences"), which makes all its jury-award-winning narrative short and animated short films eligible for an Academy Award.

### African festivals

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Film_festival&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: African festivals")]

Further information: [List of film festivals § Africa](/wiki/List_of_film_festivals#Africa "List of film festivals")

Several significant film festivals are held regularly in Africa. The [Cairo International Film Festival](/wiki/Cairo_International_Film_Festival "Cairo International Film Festival") in Cairo was established in 1976, the biannual [Panafrican Film and Television Festival of Ouagadougou](/wiki/Panafrican_Film_and_Television_Festival_of_Ouagadougou "Panafrican Film and Television Festival of Ouagadougou") (FESPACO) in [Burkina Faso](/wiki/Burkina_Faso "Burkina Faso") was established in 1969 and accepts competition-only films by African filmmakers and chiefly produced in Africa. The annual [Durban International Film Festival](/wiki/Durban_International_Film_Festival "Durban International Film Festival") in [South Africa](/wiki/South_Africa "South Africa") and [Zanzibar International Film Festival](/wiki/Zanzibar_International_Film_Festival "Zanzibar International Film Festival") in [Tanzania](/wiki/Tanzania "Tanzania") have grown in importance for the film and entertainment industry, as they often screen the African premieres of many international films. The Nairobi Film Festival (NBO), established in 2016 with a special focus on screening exceptional films from around the world that are rarely presented in Nairobi's mainstream cinema, has also grown in popularity over the years, improving the cinema-going culture in Kenya.[[54]](#cite_note-54)

The [Sahara International Film Festival](/wiki/Sahara_International_Film_Festival "Sahara International Film Festival"), held annually in the [Sahrawi refugee camps](/wiki/Sahrawi_refugee_camps "Sahrawi refugee camps") in western Algeria near the border of [Western Sahara](/wiki/Western_Sahara "Western Sahara"), is notable as the only film festival in the world to take place in a refugee camp. The festival aims to provide cultural entertainment and educational opportunities to refugees and raise awareness of the plight of the Sahrawi people, who have been exiled from their native Western Sahara for more than three decades.

### Asian film festivals

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Film_festival&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: Asian film festivals")]

#### India

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Film_festival&action=edit&section=16 "Edit section: India")]

The [International Film Festival of India](/wiki/International_Film_Festival_of_India "International Film Festival of India") in [Goa](/wiki/Goa "Goa"), organized by the government of India, was founded in 1952.[[55]](#cite_note-55)

[Chennai International Film Festival](/wiki/Chennai_International_Film_Festival "Chennai International Film Festival") has been organized since 2002 by the Indo Cine Appreciation Foundation (ICAF), the [Government of Tamil Nadu](/wiki/Government_of_Tamil_Nadu "Government of Tamil Nadu"), the [South Indian Film Chamber of Commerce](/wiki/South_Indian_Film_Chamber_of_Commerce "South Indian Film Chamber of Commerce"), and the [Film Federation of India](/wiki/Film_Federation_of_India "Film Federation of India").[[56]](#cite_note-56)

The [Jaipur International Film Festival](/wiki/Jaipur_International_Film_Festival "Jaipur International Film Festival") was founded in 2009[[57]](#cite_note-57) and [International Film Festival of Kerala](/wiki/International_Film_Festival_of_Kerala "International Film Festival of Kerala") organized by the Government of Kerala held annually at [Thiruvananthapuram](/wiki/Thiruvananthapuram "Thiruvananthapuram").[[58]](#cite_note-58)

The [International Documentary and Short Film Festival of Kerala](/wiki/International_Documentary_and_Short_Film_Festival_of_Kerala "International Documentary and Short Film Festival of Kerala") (IDSFFK), hosted by the Kerala State Chalachitra Academy, is a major documentary and short film festival.[[59]](#cite_note-59)

The [Mumbai Women's International Film Festival](/wiki/Mumbai_Women%27s_International_Film_Festival "Mumbai Women's International Film Festival") (MWIFF) is an annual film festival in Mumbai that features films made by women directors and technicians.

The Calcutta International Cult Films Festival (CICFF) is a popular international film festival based in Kolkata which showcases international cult films.

YathaKatha International Film & Literature Festival (YKIFLF) is an annual film and literature festival in [Mumbai](/wiki/Mumbai "Mumbai") showcasing literature collaboration in cinema via various constructive discussions and forums. 3rd edition of the festival was held from 28 November-1 December 2024 in Mumbai, [Maharashtra](/wiki/Maharashtra "Maharashtra"), India.[[60]](#cite_note-60)[[61]](#cite_note-61)[[62]](#cite_note-62)[[63]](#cite_note-63)

#### Others

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Film_festival&action=edit&section=17 "Edit section: Others")]

Notable festivals include the [Hong Kong International Film Festival](/wiki/Hong_Kong_International_Film_Festival "Hong Kong International Film Festival") ([HKIFF](/wiki/HKIFF "HKIFF")), [Busan International Film Festival](/wiki/Busan_International_Film_Festival "Busan International Film Festival") (BIFF), [Kathmandu International Mountain Film Festival](/wiki/Kathmandu_International_Mountain_Film_Festival "Kathmandu International Mountain Film Festival"), [Melbourne International Film Festival](/wiki/Melbourne_International_Film_Festival "Melbourne International Film Festival") (MIFF) and World Film Carnival Singapore.

### Arab World film festivals

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Film_festival&action=edit&section=18 "Edit section: Arab World film festivals")]

Further information: [Arab film festivals](/wiki/Arab_film_festivals "Arab film festivals")

There are several major film festivals in the [Arab world](/wiki/Arab_world "Arab world"), such as the Beirut International Film Festival, [Cairo International Film Festival](/wiki/Cairo_International_Film_Festival "Cairo International Film Festival"), the only international competitive feature film festival recognized by the [FIAPF](/wiki/FIAPF "FIAPF") in the Arab world and Africa, as well as the oldest in this category, [Carthage Film Festival](/wiki/Carthage_Film_Festival "Carthage Film Festival"), the oldest festival in Africa and the Arab world, [Alexandria International Film Festival](/wiki/Alexandria_International_Film_Festival "Alexandria International Film Festival"), and [Marrakech International Film Festival](/wiki/Marrakech_International_Film_Festival "Marrakech International Film Festival").

### Business model

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Film_festival&action=edit&section=20 "Edit section: Business model")]

Although there are notable for-profit festivals such as [SXSW](/wiki/South_by_Southwest "South by Southwest"), most festivals operate on a nonprofit membership-based model, with a combination of ticket sales, membership fees, and corporate sponsorship constituting the majority of revenue. Unlike other arts nonprofits (performing arts, museums, etc.), film festivals typically receive few donations from the general public and are occasionally organized as nonprofit business associations instead of public charities. Film industry members often have significant curatorial input, and corporate sponsors are given opportunities to promote their brand to festival audiences in exchange for cash contributions. Private parties, usually to raise investments for film projects, constitute significant "fringe" events. More prominent festivals often maintain year-round staff who frequently engage in community and charitable projects outside the festival season.

### Entry fee

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Film_festival&action=edit&section=21 "Edit section: Entry fee")]

While entries from established film professionals are usually considered pluses by the organizers, most festivals require new or relatively unknown filmmakers to pay an entry fee to have their works considered for screening. This is especially so in more significant film festivals, such as the Calcutta International Cult Films Festival, [Jaipur International Film Festival](/wiki/Jaipur_International_Film_Festival "Jaipur International Film Festival"), [Toronto International Film Festival](/wiki/Toronto_International_Film_Festival "Toronto International Film Festival"), [Sundance Film Festival](/wiki/Sundance_Film_Festival "Sundance Film Festival"), [South by Southwest](/wiki/South_by_Southwest "South by Southwest"), [Montreal World Film Festival](/wiki/Montreal_World_Film_Festival "Montreal World Film Festival"), and even smaller "boutique" festivals such as the [Miami International Film Festival](/wiki/Miami_International_Film_Festival "Miami International Film Festival"), [British Urban Film Festival](/wiki/British_Urban_Film_Festival "British Urban Film Festival") and [Mumbai Women's International Film Festival](/wiki/Mumbai_Women%27s_International_Film_Festival "Mumbai Women's International Film Festival").

On the other hand, some festivals—usually those accepting fewer films and perhaps not attracting as many big names in their audiences as do [Sundance](/wiki/Sundance_Film_Festival "Sundance Film Festival") and [Telluride](/wiki/Telluride_Film_Festival "Telluride Film Festival"), require no entry fee. Many smaller film festivals in the United States, such as the [Stony Brook Film Festival](/wiki/Stony_Brook_Film_Festival "Stony Brook Film Festival") on [Long Island](/wiki/Long_Island "Long Island"), the [Northwest Filmmakers' Festival](/wiki/Northwest_Filmmakers%27_Festival "Northwest Filmmakers' Festival"), and the Sicilian Film Festival in [Miami](/wiki/Miami "Miami")), are examples.

The [Portland International Film Festival](/wiki/Portland_International_Film_Festival "Portland International Film Festival") charges an entry fee but waives it for filmmakers from the [Northwestern United States](/wiki/Northwestern_United_States "Northwestern United States"), and some others with regional focuses have similar approaches.

Several film festival submission portal websites exist to streamline filmmakers' entries into multiple festivals. They provide databases of festival calls for entry and offer filmmakers a convenient "describe once, submit many" service.

### Screening out of competition

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Film_festival&action=edit&section=22 "Edit section: Screening out of competition")]

The core tradition of film festivals is competition, or judging which films most deserve various forms of recognition. Some festivals, such as the famous [Cannes Film Festival](/wiki/Cannes_Film_Festival "Cannes Film Festival"), may screen films that are considered close to competition-quality without being included in the competition; the films are said to be screened "out of competition".
